title: TRIS buffer
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e624a","text":"This protocol describes how to make TRIS buffer (TRIS-HCl) at a 1M concentration and a pH 8.0","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bt2fl","text":"This protocol describes how to achieve a TRIS buffer at a pH 8.0. If a lower pH is required, i.e. pH 7.4, then add more hydrochloric acid until desired pH is reached","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bobjh","text":"Read SDS for TRIS base and Hydrochloric acid prior to use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 121.14g of TRIS base to 800 mL of triple distilled H₂O
Stir solution on magnetic plate using magnetic flea until TRIS base completely dissolved
Using a pH meter, adjust pH of solution to pH 8.0 by adding concentrated hydrochloric acid (HCl)
Add triple distilled H₂O to bring final volume of solution to 1 L
creator: Katy M Monteith
